actionUnits.condition: Precondition: The Fire MonoBehaviour on this object (component fileID 2592690533154406537, script guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) uses the serialized settings in the scene: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60 (timeToOn randomized in Awake), onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance≈3.5-4. Ensure at least two neighboring Fire instances within maxNeighborDistance are already on and that FindNeighbors(...) has been called by the scene/controller so onNeighbors reflects reality. Expected behavior: CheckNeighborState() sets shouldTurnOn=true; after timeToOn elapses, Update() sets isOn=true and UpdateFireState(true) will Play the particle system, notify neighbors, and call FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1). This Trigger waits long enough for the turn-on to occur.

Precondition: Fire is currently ON (e.g., as a result of the previous step). During this trigger window, an external water system (e.g., hose/particle) must invoke IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) on this component at t=0s, 1.5s, 3.0s, and 4.5s (each interval < maxHitInterval=2). Serialized values: maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5. Expected behavior: Update() remains in isTurningOff state across hits, currentHitTime accumulates to >= totalHitTime, then fire turns OFF (isOn=false), shouldTurnOn=false, timers reset, UpdateFireState(true) stops particles and calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Cancellation path validation. Precondition: Fire is ON. At the start of this trigger, the water system invokes a single IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) call, then makes no further hits. Expected behavior: Update() sets isTurningOff=true and starts timers; when currentHitInterval reaches maxHitInterval=2 without a new hit, isTurningOff becomes false and currentHitTime resets to 0 (no turn-off occurs). Fire remains ON; no UpdateFireState(false/true) change is triggered here.
